card_description: Lord Krishna is a historical figure referenced in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. Appears in 2 Exegesis passages. Context: "Dick concludes his massive day of theorizing by realizing the 1st Advent is an "Eternal Now" (always occurring at the vernal equinox) and the "Rome" memories were incidentals of an "Internal Epiphany."

linked_segments.summary: Dick concludes his massive day of theorizing by realizing the 1st Advent is an "Eternal Now" (always occurring at the vernal equinox) and the "Rome" memories were incidentals of an "Internal Epiphany.
linked_segments.title: 1976-09-15_Dorothy_16.txt

linked_segments.summary: Dick identifies his 1974 ordeal with the Xerox letter as a "Great Assize" or trial of choice. By choosing "correctly" (choosing the light), he believes he cancelled his "Karmic Burden" (Sin) and obtai
linked_segments.title: 1976-09-15_Dorothy_14.txt
